content.method: Longitudinal administrative-data linkage following cohorts from age-10/12 selection through tertiary entry and early labor-market outcomes, using matching or instrumental-variable designs (e.g., admission-threshold discontinuities) to separate institutional value-added from selection/composition effects.
content.question: Do short-term value-added gains for students selected into 6/8-year gimnazium tracks persist into upper-secondary completion, tertiary attainment, and labor-market outcomes, or do they dissipate once background and peer effects are accounted for?
content.why_it_matters: This bears directly on whether T5 (quotas/outreach) delivers genuine long-run benefit to newly admitted disadvantaged students, and whether the entire track system's claimed value proposition justifies its equity costs — contested findings here (including a reported null tertiary effect) create real uncertainty about the core rationale for preserving or expanding access to tracks.
